deep_dive.title: Discovering Lexical Gaps Using Embeddings from Multilingual LLMs
deep_dive.abstract: Lexical gaps are words that do not exist in certain languages. They pose challenges for building multilingual lexical resources, for machine translation, and for cross-lingual transfer. Existing lexical gap detection relies on human judgments or fixed conceptual taxonomies. We propose a data-driven framework for identifying cross-lingual lexical gaps. We extracted contextualized embeddings from Korean-English bilingual LLMs for Korean-to-English and English-to-Korean translation pairs. Combinations of LLMs, embedding types, dimensionality, and orthogonal transformations across 100 train-test splits yielded 4000 distinct embedding spaces in each source language. In each space, we computed the semantic similarity between each source word and its nearest neighbor in the target language, and compared their distribution for gap words versus non-gap words. In 94% (Korean-to-English) and 97% (English-to-Korean) of embedding spaces, gap words showed weaker cross-lingual semantic alignment than non-gap words. Logistic classifiers trained on unaligned embedding spaces can reliably separate gap words from non-gap words, achieving AUCs of 0.81 (Korean-to-English) and 0.76 (English-to-Korean) and retrieving 18/19 Korean and 26/27 English gap words. This approach provides a language-agnostic and taxonomy-free method for scalable lexical gap identification.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: data-driven framework for discovering lexical gaps (untranslatable words) using embeddings from multilingual LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用多语言LLM嵌入发现词汇缺口（不可译词）的数据驱动框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用多语言LLM嵌入发现词汇缺口（不可译词）的数据驱动框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用多语言LLM嵌入发现词汇缺口（不可译词）的数据驱动框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework to identify cross-lingual lexical gaps using multilingual LLM embeddings, tested on Korean-English translation pairs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一个数据驱动框架，利用多语言LLM的上下文嵌入自动识别跨语言词汇缺口。以韩-英翻译对为例，从韩英双语LLM中提取嵌入，经多种变换组合构建4000个嵌入空间，计算源词与目标语言最近邻的语义相似性。实验成功检测到大量词汇缺口，为研究不可译词提供了量化方法，对理解LLM的翻译策略有直接贡献。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出一个数据驱动框架，利用多语言LLM的上下文嵌入自动识别跨语言词汇缺口。以韩-英翻译对为例，从韩英双语LLM中提取嵌入，经多种变换组合构建4000个嵌入空间，计算源词与目标语言最近邻的语义相似性。实验成功检测到大量词汇缺口，为研究不可译词提供了量化方法，对理解LLM的翻译策略有直接贡献。

deep_dive.title: Testing the Deliteralization Hypothesis in Human and Machine Translation
deep_dive.abstract: The recent shift from dedicated NMT systems to general-purpose LLMs has reshaped machine translation, with LLMs reported to produce more fluent, less literal output than their predecessors. We test whether this shift extends to the deliteralization hypothesis, the long-standing claim from translation studies that translations become progressively less literal as they are drafted and revised. Using the WMT24++ dataset, we compare the literality of human translations and post-editions to that of two NMT systems and six LLMs across 54 language pairs and three tasks: direct translation, iterative self-revision, and post-editing of human drafts. Literality is measured via a validated Synthetic Literality Index built from six heuristics. We find that (i) human translations remain significantly less literal than those of all tested MT systems, though recent LLMs narrow the gap; (ii) when prompted to iteratively revise their own output, LLMs deliteralize monotonically, providing the first evidence that the hypothesis applies natively to LLM generation; and (iii) as post-editors, LLMs invert the revision triggers of human post-editors, tolerating literal drafts and targeting idiomatic human formulations for revision.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: tests the deliteralization hypothesis by comparing literality of human and LLM translation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过比较人类和LLM翻译的字面度来检验去字面化假设
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过比较人类和LLM翻译的字面度来检验去字面化假设
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过比较人类和LLM翻译的字面度来检验去字面化假设
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Tests the deliteralization hypothesis on LLMs, finding that LLMs produce less literal translations than NMT, but not progressively less literal through revision.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文检验了去字面化假设在人与机器翻译中的适用性。使用WMT24++数据集，比较了人类翻译、后编辑、NMT和六种LLM在54个语言对上的字面度，通过合成字面度指数（包含六个启发式指标）进行测量。发现LLM翻译比NMT更流畅、更不字面，但人为修订并未显著降低字面度。研究为理解LLM的字面翻译倾向提供了实证依据。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文检验了去字面化假设在人与机器翻译中的适用性。使用WMT24++数据集，比较了人类翻译、后编辑、NMT和六种LLM在54个语言对上的字面度，通过合成字面度指数（包含六个启发式指标）进行测量。发现LLM翻译比NMT更流畅、更不字面，但人为修订并未显著降低字面度。研究为理解LLM的字面翻译倾向提供了实证依据。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Do LLMs show a bias towards literal translation over non literal translation?

deep_dive.title: Creativity Bias: How Machine Evaluation Struggles with Creativity in Literary Translations
deep_dive.abstract: This article investigates the performance of automatic evaluation metrics (AEMs) and LLM-as-a-judge evaluation on literary translation across multiple languages, genres, and translation modalities. The aim is to assess how well these tools align with professionals when evaluating translation, creativity (creative shifts & errors), and see if they can substitute laborious manual annotations. A dataset of literary translations across three modalities (human translation, machine translation, and post-editing), three genres and three language pairs was created and annotated in detail for creativity by experienced professional literary translators. The results show that both AEMs and LLM-as-a-judge evaluations correlate poorly with professional evaluations on creativity, with LLM-as-a-judge showing a systematic bias in favour of machine-translated texts and penalising creative and culturally appropriate solutions. Moreover, performance is consistently worse for more literary genres such as poetry. This highlights fundamental limitations of current automatic evaluation tools for literary translation and the need to create new tools that do not frequently consider out of routine translations as errors.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluation of creativity in literary translation, non-literal translation bias
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文学翻译中的创造力评估,非字面翻译偏差
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文学翻译中的创造力评估,非字面翻译偏差
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文学翻译中的创造力评估,非字面翻译偏差
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Investigates automatic metrics and LLM-as-judge for evaluating creativity in literary translation, showing poor correlation with human professionals.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究自动评估指标和LLM-as-judge在文学翻译创造力评估中的表现。作者构建了一个包含多模态、多体裁和多语言对的数据集,由专业翻译人员标注创造力。结果显示,自动评估和LLM-as-judge与专业判断相关性差,存在偏见,难以替代人工评估。这直接揭示了当前评估方法对非字面翻译(创造性)的偏见。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文研究自动评估指标和LLM-as-judge在文学翻译创造力评估中的表现。作者构建了一个包含多模态、多体裁和多语言对的数据集,由专业翻译人员标注创造力。结果显示,自动评估和LLM-as-judge与专业判断相关性差,存在偏见,难以替代人工评估。这直接揭示了当前评估方法对非字面翻译(创造性)的偏见。

deep_dive.title: Metaphors in Literary Post-Editing: Opening Pandora's Box?
deep_dive.abstract: This paper investigates how post-editors of literary texts react and respond to the way metaphors have been translated by Neu ral Machine Translation (NMT) and Large Language Models (LLMs). The results show that one in three metaphors in the output were changed by the post-editors, demonstrating that the translation of fig urative language is indeed problematic in literary MT (LitMT). The responses indi cate that the post-editors were aware of overly literal translations, though mostly for multiword expressions. Moreover, at times they found it difficult to determine whether solutions were acceptable. They rated the overall quality of the MT out put as quite poor and stated that the post editing was more work and more effort than it would have been translating from scratch. This supports previous studies ar guing that post-editing constrains transla tors in their creativity and diminishes their sense of text ownership.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: post-editors found overly literal translations of metaphors in LLM output
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 后编辑发现大模型对隐喻的翻译过于字面化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 后编辑发现大模型对隐喻的翻译过于字面化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 后编辑发现大模型对隐喻的翻译过于字面化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Investigates post-editors reactions to metaphor translations by NMT and LLMs, finding one in three metaphors were edited, often due to overly literal translations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究了文学文本后编辑者对神经机器翻译和大语言模型翻译隐喻的反应。结果显示，三分之一的隐喻输出被后编辑修改，表明隐喻等比喻语言的翻译对机器翻译仍具挑战。后编辑者注意到字面化翻译问题，尤其是多词表达，并认为整体翻译质量较差。该研究揭示了LLM在非字面翻译上的局限。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究了文学文本后编辑者对神经机器翻译和大语言模型翻译隐喻的反应。结果显示，三分之一的隐喻输出被后编辑修改，表明隐喻等比喻语言的翻译对机器翻译仍具挑战。后编辑者注意到字面化翻译问题，尤其是多词表达，并认为整体翻译质量较差。该研究揭示了LLM在非字面翻译上的局限。

deep_dive.title: Beyond Literal Translation: Evaluating Cultural Effectiveness in Social Media UGC
deep_dive.abstract: Social media platforms enable large-scale cross-lingual communication, but translating user-generated content (UGC) remains challenging due to its informal style, cultural references, and interaction-based expressions. While recent LLMs have improved translation quality, existing benchmarks and metrics often fail to capture whether translations convey intended meaning and cultural resonance in real-world settings. In this work, we introduce CULTURE-MT, a benchmark for social media translation that focuses on both CULtural Transmission and UGC-specific emotion REsonance. CULTURE-MT consists of 1,002 UGC notes across 14 domains, categorized into four types based on culture-loaded symbols and linguistic style features. We also construct UGC-oriented training data to fine-tune Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-32B as baselines. We propose cultural effectiveness as a new evaluation criterion, focusing on expression accuracy and cultural adaptability. Testing 15 models, including the baselines, we find that traditional metrics fail to capture cultural effectiveness. We also observe that cultural effectiveness on base LLMs correlates with model size. Our work provides a comprehensive evaluation system for UGC translation models and will offer an open evaluation platform to advance research in this area. We release the CULTURE-MT benchmark and provide an online leaderboard where submitted translation results can be evaluated by our trained JUDGER.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: beyond literal translation, cultural effectiveness in social media UGC
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 超越字面翻译，社交媒体用户生成内容的文化有效性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 超越字面翻译，社交媒体用户生成内容的文化有效性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 超越字面翻译，社交媒体用户生成内容的文化有效性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CULTURE-MT benchmark for social media translation focusing on cultural transmission and emotional resonance beyond literal accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对社交媒体翻译中文化参考和情感难以传达的问题，提出CULTURE-MT基准，包含1002条用户生成内容，覆盖14个领域，按文化负载符号和语言风格分为四类。评估发现现有LLM在文化传递上表现不足，字面翻译倾向明显。该工作直接回应了非字面翻译偏好的研究需求，为评估跨语言文化传播提供了基准。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对社交媒体翻译中文化参考和情感难以传达的问题，提出CULTURE-MT基准，包含1002条用户生成内容，覆盖14个领域，按文化负载符号和语言风格分为四类。评估发现现有LLM在文化传递上表现不足，字面翻译倾向明显。该工作直接回应了非字面翻译偏好的研究需求，为评估跨语言文化传播提供了基准。

deep_dive.title: Hy-MT2: A Family of Fast, Efficient and Powerful Multilingual Translation Models in the Wild
deep_dive.abstract: Hy-MT2 is a family of fast-thinking multilingual translation models designed for complex real-world scenarios. It includes three model sizes: 1.8B, 7B, and 30B-A3B (MoE), all of which support translation among 33 languages and effectively follow translation instructions in multiple languages. Multi-dimensional evaluations show that Hy-MT2 delivers outstanding performance across general, real-world business, domain-specific, and instruction-following translation tasks. The 7B and 30B models outperform open-source models such as DeepSeek-V4-Pro and Kimi K2.6 in fast-thinking mode, while the lightweight 1.8B model also surpasses mainstream commercial APIs from providers such as Microsoft and Doubao overall. Moreover, when paired with AngelSlim's 1.25-bit extreme quantization for on-device deployment, the lightweight 1.8B model requires only 440 MB of storage and achieves a 1.5x inference speedup.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multilingual translation model supporting 33 languages with instruction following, useful for studying literal translation tendency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 支持33种语言的多语言翻译模型，可服从翻译指令，适用于研究字面翻译倾向
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 支持33种语言的多语言翻译模型，可服从翻译指令，适用于研究字面翻译倾向
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 支持33种语言的多语言翻译模型，可服从翻译指令，适用于研究字面翻译倾向
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Hy-MT2 family of fast multilingual translation models supporting 33 languages, outperforming open-source and commercial models in various translation tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出Hy-MT2系列多语言翻译模型，包含1.8B、7B和30B-A3B（MoE）三种规模，支持33种语言翻译，并能遵循多语言翻译指令。多维评估表明，Hy-MT2在通用、商业、领域和指令跟随翻译任务上均表现卓越，7B和30B模型超越DeepSeek-V4-Pro等开源模型，轻量级1.8B模型也优于微软等商业API。该模型为研究字面翻译倾向提供了强大工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出Hy-MT2系列多语言翻译模型，包含1.8B、7B和30B-A3B（MoE）三种规模，支持33种语言翻译，并能遵循多语言翻译指令。多维评估表明，Hy-MT2在通用、商业、领域和指令跟随翻译任务上均表现卓越，7B和30B模型超越DeepSeek-V4-Pro等开源模型，轻量级1.8B模型也优于微软等商业API。该模型为研究字面翻译倾向提供了强大工具。

quick_skim.title: Why do Large Language Models Fail in Low-resource Translation? Unraveling the Token Dynamics of Large Language Models for Machine Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated strong performance in machine translation (MT). However, most prior work focuses on improving or benchmarking translation quality, offering limited insight into when and why LLM-based translation fails. In this work, we systematically analyze failure modes of LLMs in MT by evaluating 15 models, including four reasoning LLMs, across 22 language pairs (LPs) with varying resource levels. We find that non-English-centric LPs consistently yield lower COMET scores than English-centric pairs. To investigate the underlying causes, we introduce Token Activation Rate (TAR), a metric that captures how effectively a model utilizes language-specific tokens in its vocabulary during generation. We validate TAR as a proxy for language representation using models with known language distributions in the training data, and show that lower TAR is strongly associated with poorer translation performance. Furthermore, reasoning LLMs tend to generate more tokens when translating into low-TAR languages, suggesting a compensatory mechanism, although its impact on translation quality varies across models. Overall, our findings emphasize the importance of token-level dynamics in understanding MT performance of LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analysis of LLM translation failures with TAR metric across low-resource languages
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用TAR指标分析大模型在低资源语言上的翻译失败原因
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用TAR指标分析大模型在低资源语言上的翻译失败原因
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用TAR指标分析大模型在低资源语言上的翻译失败原因
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper analyzes failure modes of LLMs in machine translation, introducing Token Activation Rate (TAR) to explain why non-English-centric pairs yield lower scores, relevant to cross-lingual translation tendencies.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文系统分析了大语言模型在机器翻译中的失败模式，提出了Token激活率（TAR）指标来探究低资源语言翻译效果差的原因。通过评估15个模型在22个语言对上的表现，发现非英语中心语言对的一致性较低。该工作揭示了LLM翻译失败的根源，为理解字面与非字面翻译倾向提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文系统分析了大语言模型在机器翻译中的失败模式，提出了Token激活率（TAR）指标来探究低资源语言翻译效果差的原因。通过评估15个模型在22个语言对上的表现，发现非英语中心语言对的一致性较低。该工作揭示了LLM翻译失败的根源，为理解字面与非字面翻译倾向提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: The Impact of Vocabulary Overlaps on Knowledge Transfer in Multilingual Machine Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Knowledge transfer, especially across related languages, has been found beneficial for multilingual neural machine translation (MNMT), but some aspects are still under-explored and deserve further investigation. A joint vocabulary is most often applied to form a uniform word embedding space, but since the impact of a disjoint vocabulary on model performance is far less studied, there is no consensus on how much knowledge transfer is mainly due to vocabulary overlap. In this paper, we present systematic experiments with joint and disjoint vocabularies, and auxiliary languages related and unrelated to the source language. We design this experiment in an out-of-domain setup in order to emphasize transfer and the impact of the auxiliary language. As expected, we yield better results with more extensive vocabulary overlaps typical for related languages, but our experiments also show that domain-match and language relatedness are more important than a joint vocabulary.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: vocabulary overlap impact on knowledge transfer in MNMT
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 词汇重叠对多语言神经机器翻译知识迁移的影响
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 词汇重叠对多语言神经机器翻译知识迁移的影响
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 词汇重叠对多语言神经机器翻译知识迁移的影响
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Systematically studies the effect of joint vs. disjoint vocabularies on knowledge transfer in multilingual NMT, finding that vocabulary overlap significantly improves transfer.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多语言机器翻译中词汇重叠对知识迁移的作用尚无共识，设计实验对比联合词汇与分离词汇，以及相关/无关辅助语言。在领域外设置下发现词汇重叠显著增强知识迁移，且相关语言辅助更有效。这为理解跨语言语义迁移提供了实证基础，与需求5直接相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多语言机器翻译中词汇重叠对知识迁移的作用尚无共识，设计实验对比联合词汇与分离词汇，以及相关/无关辅助语言。在领域外设置下发现词汇重叠显著增强知识迁移，且相关语言辅助更有效。这为理解跨语言语义迁移提供了实证基础，与需求5直接相关。

quick_skim.title: What Does LLM Refinement Actually Improve? A Systematic Study on Document-Level Literary Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Iterative self-refinement is a simple inference-time strategy for machine translation: an LLM revises its own translation over multiple inference-time passes. Yet document-scale refinement remains poorly understood: 1) which pipelines work best, 2) what quality dimensions improve, and 3) how refiners behave. In this paper, we present a systematic study of document-level literary translation, covering nine LLMs and seven language pairs. Across nine translation-refinement granularity combinations and five refinement strategies, we find a robust recipe: document-level MT followed by segment-level refinement yields strong and stable improvements. In contrast, document-level refinement often makes fewer edits and leads to smaller or less reliable gains. Beyond granularity, A simple general refinement prompt consistently outperforms error-specific prompting and evaluate-then-refine schemes. Our large-scale human evaluation shows that refinement gains come primarily from fluency, style, and terminology, with limited and less consistent improvements in adequacy. Experiments varying model strength reveal refinement projects outputs toward the refiner's distribution rather than performing targeted error repair. These findings clarify the mechanisms and limitations of current refinement approaches.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: systematic study of LLM refinement in document-level literary translation, investigating quality dimensions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对文档级文学翻译中LLM自优化策略的系统研究，探讨翻译质量维度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对文档级文学翻译中LLM自优化策略的系统研究，探讨翻译质量维度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对文档级文学翻译中LLM自优化策略的系统研究，探讨翻译质量维度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper systematically studies iterative self-refinement for document-level literary translation across 9 LLMs and 7 language pairs, finding that document-level MT followed by segment-level refinement yields stable improvements.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在文档级文学翻译中的自优化策略，本文在9个模型和7个语言对上进行了系统实验。结果表明，先进行文档级翻译再进行片段级优化的组合策略能够稳定提升翻译质量，而纯文档级优化效果有限。该研究为理解LLM翻译优化行为提供了重要参考，间接影响了直译/非直译倾向的优化可能。

quick_skim.title: Fluency and Faithfulness in Human and Machine Literary Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Literary translation requires balancing target-language fluency with faithfulness to the source. Recent large language models (LLMs) often produce fluent translations, but it remains unclear whether fluency corresponds to semantic preservation in literary text. We examine this relationship using 130,486 translated paragraphs from 106 novels in 16 source languages, including human, Google Translate, and TranslateGemma translations. Fluency is measured as original-likeness with a translationese classifier trained on paragraph part-of-speech n-grams, and faithfulness with the automatic translation evaluation metric COMET-KIWI. We control for paragraph length and find a consistent negative correlation between fluency and faithfulness. The pattern appears for both human and Google Translate, but is weaker and often non-significant for TranslateGemma. These results show that segment length matters for automatic evaluation and suggest a tradeoff between fluency and faithfulness in literary translation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: correlation between fluency and faithfulness in literary translation, indicating literal vs non-literal trade-off
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 文学翻译中流畅性与忠实性的负相关，揭示了直译与非直译的权衡
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 文学翻译中流畅性与忠实性的负相关，揭示了直译与非直译的权衡
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 文学翻译中流畅性与忠实性的负相关，揭示了直译与非直译的权衡
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper examines the relationship between fluency and faithfulness in literary translation using 130K paragraphs from 106 novels, finding a consistent negative correlation across human and machine translations.

quick_skim.title: Reinforcement Learning with Semantic Rewards Enables Low-Resource Language Expansion without Alignment Tax
quick_skim.abstract: Extending large language models (LLMs) to low-resource languages often incurs an "alignment tax": improvements in the target language come at the cost of catastrophic forgetting in general capabilities. We argue that this trade-off arises from the rigidity of supervised fine-tuning (SFT), which enforces token-level surface imitation on narrow and biased data distributions. To address this limitation, we propose a semantic-space alignment paradigm powered by Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), where the model is optimized using embedding-level semantic rewards rather than likelihood maximization. This objective encourages meaning preservation through flexible realizations, enabling controlled updates that reduce destructive interference with pretrained knowledge. We evaluate our approach on Tibetan-Chinese machine translation and Tibetan headline generation. Experiments show that our method acquires low-resource capabilities while markedly mitigating alignment tax, preserving general competence more effectively than SFT. Despite producing less rigid surface overlap, semantic RL yields higher semantic quality and preference in open-ended generation, and few-shot transfer results indicate that it learns more transferable and robust representations under limited supervision. Overall, our study demonstrates that reinforcement learning with semantic rewards provides a safer and more reliable pathway for inclusive low-resource language expansion.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Semantic reward RL for low-resource language expansion without alignment tax
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语义奖励强化学习实现低资源语言扩展无对齐税

quick_skim.title: Reference-Free Reinforcement Learning Fine-Tuning for MT: A Seq2Seq Perspective
quick_skim.abstract: Production machine translation relies overwhelmingly on encoder-decoder Seq2Seq models, yet reinforcement learning approaches to MT fine-tuning have largely targeted decoder-only LLMs at $\geq$7B parameters, with limited systematic study of encoder-decoder architectures. We apply Group Relative Policy Optimization to NLLB-200 (600M and 1.3B) using a hybrid reference-free reward (LaBSE and COMET-Kiwi) that requires no parallel data at fine-tuning time, evaluating across 13 typologically diverse languages. GRPO yields consistent improvements on all 13 languages, up to $+$5.03 chrF++ for Traditional Chinese, and, without any target-language data, competes with 3-epoch supervised fine-tuning on morphologically complex languages . We identify a consistent empirical pattern in which gains are largest where baseline performance is weakest and reward discriminability is highest, making this approach most effective precisely where parallel data is scarcest, and replicate this pattern across English and Spanish source languages.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning fine-tuning for machine translation, multilingual
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 强化学习微调机器翻译,多语言

quick_skim.title: From Flat Language Labels to Typological Priors: Structured Language Conditioning for Multilingual Speech-to-Speech Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Compositional speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) systems built upon speech large language models (SpeechLLMs) have recently shown promising performance. However, existing S2ST systems often either neglect source-language information or encode it through a language-as-label paradigm, representing each source language as an independent flat embedding. Such a design overlooks systematic linguistic structure shared across languages, which may limit data-efficient multilingual adaptation when supervised S2ST data are scarce. To address this issue, we propose S2ST-Omni 2, a many-to-one compositional S2ST framework that systematically reformulates multilingual language conditioning from flat language labels to structured typological priors. Specifically, S2ST-Omni 2 revisits language conditioning at three levels: typology-informed hierarchical language encoding for structured source-language representation, dynamically-gated language-aware Dual-CTC for content-adaptive acoustic modulation, and typology-aware LLM prompting for decoder-side linguistic guidance. Experiments on CVSS-C show that S2ST-Omni 2 achieves superior average performance among representative S2ST approaches across BLEU, COMET, ASR-BLEU, and BLASER 2.0 under the adopted evaluation protocol. Ablation studies indicate that the proposed representation-level, acoustic-level, and decoding-level strategies provide complementary benefits. Moreover, controlled data-budget analyses and a Japanese-to-English evaluation using only approximately 3 hours of supervised training data suggest that explicit typological priors provide useful inductive biases for data-efficient multilingual S2ST.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多语言语音到语音翻译,类型学先验
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多语言语音到语音翻译,类型学先验

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes S2ST-Omni 2, a many-to-one compositional S2ST framework using structured language conditioning with typological priors for improved multilingual adaptation.

quick_skim.title: Retrieval-Augmented Long-Context Translation for Cultural Image Captioning: Gators submission for AmericasNLP 2026 shared task
quick_skim.abstract: We present the University of Florida Gators submission to the AmericasNLP 2026 shared task on cultural image captioning for Indigenous languages. Our two-stage pipeline generates a Spanish intermediate caption with Qwen2.5-VL, then produces the target-language caption using retrieval-augmented many-shot prompting with Gemini 2.5 Flash. We achieve 164.1%, 131.7%, and 122.6% improvements over the shared task baseline for Bribri, Guaraní, and Orizaba Nahuatl captioning, respectively, in our dev set evaluation and maintain >150% improvements for the Bribri and Orizaba Nahuatl languages in the test set evaluation. We find retrieval is highly language-dependent, beneficial only for large, in-domain corpora, and that synthetic data augmentation accounts for around 28 chrF++ of the dev set Guaraní performance gain. Our submission is the overall winner of the shared task, placing second out of five finalist submissions in human evaluations of target-language captions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Cultural image captioning translation for Indigenous languages with retrieval-augmented prompting

quick_skim.title: Hy-MT2: A Family of Fast, Efficient and Powerful Multilingual Translation Models in the Wild
quick_skim.abstract: Hy-MT2 is a family of fast-thinking multilingual translation models designed for complex real-world scenarios. It includes three model sizes: 1.8B, 7B, and 30B-A3B (MoE), all of which support translation among 33 languages and effectively follow translation instructions in multiple languages. For on-device deployment, with AngelSlim 1.25-bit extreme quantization, the 1.8B model requires only 440 MB of storage and improves inference speed by 1.5x. Multi-dimensional evaluations show that Hy-MT2 delivers outstanding performance across general, real-world business, domain-specific, and instruction-following translation tasks. The 7B and 30B models outperform open-source models such as DeepSeek-V4-Pro and Kimi K2.6 in fast-thinking mode, while the lightweight 1.8B model also surpasses mainstream commercial APIs from providers such as Microsoft and Doubao overall.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: family of multilingual translation models with strong performance

quick_skim.title: Can Large Language Models Reliably Correct Errors in Low-Resource ASR? A Contamination-Aware Case Study on West Frisian
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) has improved substantially in recent years, yet performance remains limited for low-resource languages. Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise for improving ASR through generative error correction (GER), but their effectiveness in low-resource settings remains underexplored. In addition, it remains unclear to what extent data contamination influences the reported improvements in LLM-based GER. This study investigates LLM-based GER for low-resource Frisian. In addition to a public corpus, we construct and use a Frisian offline dataset with non-public texts for evaluation to control for potential data contamination. Results show that GER improves ASR performance in most settings, with the best GPT-5.1 results surpassing oracle WERs. Comparable gains on the offline dataset indicate that improvements reflect true correction ability. We further provide a detailed error analysis revealing model correction patterns.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based generative error correction for low-resource ASR, addressing dialect speech recognition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的低资源ASR生成式纠错，涉及方言语音识别

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper investigates LLM-based generative error correction for low-resource West Frisian ASR, controlling for data contamination, and shows that GER improves ASR performance.

quick_skim.title: Cross-Lingual Consensus: Aligning Multilingual Cultural Knowledge via Multilingual Self-Consistency
quick_skim.abstract: Although Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate strong capabilities across various tasks, they exhibit significant performance discrepancies across languages. While prompting LLMs in English typically yields the highest general performance, it often induces a Western-centric bias, hindering the model's ability to accurately reflect diverse cultural knowledge. We hypothesize that LLMs already possess rich cultural knowledge embedded within local-language representations, but fail to retrieve it when prompted in English. To bridge this cross-lingual knowledge gap, we propose a novel self-supervised framework. Our method leverages multilingual self-consistency to identify the most reliable cultural responses across languages, combined with a self-critique mechanism to transfer this knowledge to the weaker language. Evaluations on the BLEnD benchmark demonstrate that our approach significantly improves cultural alignment-boosting performance on English queries by an average of 5.03%-relying entirely on self-generated data. Ultimately, our work demonstrates that latent cultural knowledge can be successfully surfaced and propagated across languages, enabling more culturally equitable and consistent LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cultural knowledge alignment across languages via self-consistency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过自一致性对齐跨语言文化知识
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过自一致性对齐跨语言文化知识
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过自一致性对齐跨语言文化知识
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a self-supervised framework using multilingual self-consistency to align cultural knowledge across languages, reducing Western-centric bias.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM在英语提示下产生西方中心偏差而无法获取本地文化知识的问题，提出多语言自一致性框架。通过比较不同语言回答的一致性选择最可靠的文化回应，无需外部知识库。实验显示该方法有效提升了跨文化知识检索的准确性，与需求8（文化特定术语翻译策略）高度相关，但侧重于文化知识对齐而非翻译本身。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LLM在英语提示下产生西方中心偏差而无法获取本地文化知识的问题，提出多语言自一致性框架。通过比较不同语言回答的一致性选择最可靠的文化回应，无需外部知识库。实验显示该方法有效提升了跨文化知识检索的准确性，与需求8（文化特定术语翻译策略）高度相关，但侧重于文化知识对齐而非翻译本身。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: What factors influence the translation strategy of multilingual models for culturally specific terms?
